source_rationale: CD144 (VE-cadherin) is a canonical endothelial marker, along with CD146 (MCAM) and CD39 (ENTPD1). CD49A, CD49C, CD9, and CD44 are adhesion molecules commonly expressed on endothelial cells.

CD34 is a marker of progenitor/stem cells, while CDH5 (VE-cadherin), VWF (von Willebrand factor), and FLI1 (an ETS transcription factor critical for endothelial development) together indicate an endothelial progenitor identity.

CD34 is a marker of progenitor/stem cells, while CDH5 (VE-cadherin), VWF (von Willebrand factor), and FLI1 (an ETS transcription factor critical for endothelial development) together indicate an endothelial progenitor identity.

AMH (anti-Müllerian hormone), FOXL2 (a key granulosa cell transcription factor), and FST (follistatin) are canonical markers of ovarian granulosa cells. BEX1 is also expressed in this lineage.

CD3G, CD2, CD8A, and CD69 are canonical T cell markers, with CD8A suggesting cytotoxic T cell identity. HLA-DRA, HLA-DQB1, CD14, and CXCR4 suggest an activated state with mixed immune cell markers, but the T cell receptor complex genes (CD3G) are most diagnostic.

CD3G, CD2, CD8A, and CD69 are canonical T cell markers, with CD8A suggesting cytotoxic T cell identity. HLA-DRA, HLA-DQB1, CD14, and CXCR4 suggest an activated state with mixed immune cell markers, but the T cell receptor complex genes (CD3G) are most diagnostic.

ZP3 (zona pellucida glycoprotein), GDF9, FIGLA, DDX4 (VASA), DAZL, DPPA3, OOSP2, and TUBB8 are all canonical oocyte and female germ cell markers. LIN28 and PRDM1 further support primordial germ cell/oocyte identity.

ACTA2, MYH11, TAGLN, and RGS5 are canonical smooth muscle/pericyte markers. MCAM (CD146) and RERGL further support perivascular mural cell identity. The co-expression of endothelial markers like CD144 and CD39 suggests a perivascular cell closely associated with endothelium.

DCN (decorin), PDGFRA, and APOE are markers of stromal/fibroblast-like cells. CD105 (endoglin), CD26, CD49E, CD54, CD55, CD62E, and FHL2 together suggest ovarian theca/stromal cells, with PDGFRA and DCN being key mesenchymal markers and CD200 indicating a specialized stromal population.
